reasoning: The Bleichenbacher PKCS#1 v1.5 oracle is real but gated behind a non-default configuration. DEFAULT_ENCRYPTION_SCHEME (line 3267) is 'pkcs1_oaep'; the vulnerable path is only activated when a caller explicitly passes encryptionScheme:'pkcs1'. The public API is fully exported ($$decryptKey / decrypt), so reachability is not blocked by visibility — but the gate is the non-default option requirement. Even when 'pkcs1' is chosen: (1) encUnPad (lines 3031-3064) uses constant-time bitwise accumulation with a single return-null for all failure modes, closing the timing sub-oracle; (2) the JS engine decrypt() (line 3408) throws 'Decryption failed' on any null result; (3) the $$decryptKey wrapper (lines 3862-3869) normalizes all exceptions to 'Error during decryption', collapsing the error-message oracle. What remains is only the binary throw-vs-return oracle observable by the calling application — which requires the application itself to expose a decryption oracle to an attacker (attacker submits chosen ciphertexts and observes success/failure at the application layer). Because exploitation requires both (a) an explicit non-default opt-in and (b) an application-layer oracle architecture, this is not default-reachable. Severity is downgraded from medium to low accordingly.
